clusters.title: Huge Russian strike on Ukraine's capital kills at least 17, officials say
clusters.neutral_headline: 'Most massive' Russian attack on Kyiv kills at least 18
bullet_summary: The death toll may rise, as emergency services said 86 people were injured, 70 of whom had been admitted to hospital
At least 13 people were killed and 86 injured, Ukrainian officials said

items.title: Huge Russian strike on Ukraine's capital kills at least 17, officials say
items.standfirst: Russia has launched its large-scale attack on Ukraine's capital to date, killing at least 17 people and wounding scores more, authorities say.
items.content: Russia has launched its large-scale attack on Ukraine's capital to date, killing at least 17 people and wounding scores more, authorities say.

items.title: At least 17 killed, dozens injured in major Russian attack on Kyiv
items.standfirst: At least 17 people were killed and 90 injured in a Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv, officials say. Aidan Stretch has the latest.
items.content: At least 17 people were killed and 90 injured in a Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv, officials say. Aidan Stretch has the latest.

items.title: 'Most massive' Russian attack on Kyiv kills at least 18
items.standfirst: Kyiv's mayor declares a day of mourning after the major drone and missile attack on the Ukrainian capital.
items.content: Kyiv's mayor declares a day of mourning after the major drone and missile attack on the Ukrainian capital.

items.title: At least 18 dead as Russia launches massive drone and missile attack on Kyiv
items.standfirst: Ukraine capital targeted as Russia faces fuel shortages following long-range strikes against its oil refineriesEurope live – latest updatesAt least 18 people have been killed and dozens injured overnight in Kyiv, local authorities have said as Russia launched its latest massive drone and missile attack on the Ukrainian capital.Fires were burning at sites across Kyiv as dawn broke on Thursday, with strikes or debris hitting residential buildings in several districts and a hotel on one of the central boulevards. The death toll may rise, as emergency services said 86 people were injured, 70 of whom had been admitted to hospital. Continue reading...
items.content: Ukraine capital targeted as Russia faces fuel shortages following long-range strikes against its oil refineriesEurope live – latest updatesAt least 18 people have been killed and dozens injured overnight in Kyiv, local authorities have said as Russia launched its latest massive drone and missile attack on the Ukrainian capital.Fires were burning at sites across Kyiv as dawn broke on Thursday, with strikes or debris hitting residential buildings in several districts and a hotel on one of the central boulevards. The death toll may rise, as emergency services said 86 people were injured, 70 of whom had been admitted to hospital. Continue reading...

items.title: Ukraine: Russia pounds Kyiv in massive drone, missile strike
items.standfirst: Explosions lit up the night sky in Kyiv in the early hours of Thursday as Russia launched a major drone and missile attack on Ukraine’s capital. At least 13 people were killed and 86 injured, Ukrainian officials said.
items.content: Explosions lit up the night sky in Kyiv in the early hours of Thursday as Russia launched a major drone and missile attack on Ukraine’s capital. At least 13 people were killed and 86 injured, Ukrainian officials said.

items.title: Russia unleashes huge bombardment of Kyiv
items.standfirst: Attack of 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones follows Zelenskyy’s warning that Moscow is preparing ‘massive strike’
items.content: Attack of 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones follows Zelenskyy’s warning that Moscow is preparing ‘massive strike’

items.title: Live: Russian missiles, drones kill residents, tear open buildings in 'massive attack' on Kyiv
items.standfirst: Russian missile and drone strikes tore open apartment buildings in Kyiv early Thursday, killing at least 18 people and wounding dozens, hours after President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Moscow was preparing a "massive attack". Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Russian missile and drone strikes tore open apartment buildings in Kyiv early Thursday, killing at least 18 people and wounding dozens, hours after President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that Moscow was preparing a "massive attack". Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

clusters.title: New York: Marriage proposal atop the Empire State Building ends in arrest
clusters.neutral_headline: New York: Marriage proposal atop the Empire State Building ends in arrest
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, CBS News, BBC Top Stories

items.title: New York: Marriage proposal atop the Empire State Building ends in arrest
items.standfirst: A couple of Russian daredevils scaled the spire of the Empire State Building on Wednesday where one of them got down on one knee to propose. The couple had earlier unfurled a banner with a message of peace atop the tower. They were later arrested by police.
items.content: A couple of Russian daredevils scaled the spire of the Empire State Building on Wednesday where one of them got down on one knee to propose. The couple had earlier unfurled a banner with a message of peace atop the tower. They were later arrested by police.

items.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers awaiting arraignment after apparently getting engaged at the top
items.standfirst: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.
items.content: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.

items.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
items.standfirst: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angela Nikolau, 33.
items.content: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angela Nikolau, 33.

items.title: Watch: Couple scale Empire State Building, get engaged and are arrested
items.standfirst: The two were atop the 1,454 ft (443m) building for at least ten minutes and displayed a large banner before climbing down.
items.content: The two were atop the 1,454 ft (443m) building for at least ten minutes and displayed a large banner before climbing down.

clusters.title: Mexico scene shows crowds that killed three during World Cup celebration
clusters.neutral_headline: Four die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Mexico scene shows crowds that killed three during World Cup celebration
items.standfirst: Two women and one man died of asphyxiation in downtown Mexico City as thousands of soccer fans celebrated their national team’s World Cup win over Ecuador.
items.content: Two women and one man died of asphyxiation in downtown Mexico City as thousands of soccer fans celebrated their national team’s World Cup win over Ecuador.

items.title: Four die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
items.standfirst: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico's capital on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.
items.content: More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico's capital on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.

items.title: Four people die in mass fan celebrations in Mexico City after World Cup victory
items.standfirst: Three of the victims died from suffocationMore than a million people gathered in Mexico CityFour people died, three ⁠from suffocation, as thousands of fans crowded Mexico City streets during World Cup celebrations, the capital’s health secretariat ⁠said in the early ⁠hours ​of Wednesday.The deaths occurred near the Angel of Independence landmark, where thousands of fans had gathered to ⁠celebrate Mexico’s 2-0 victory over Ecuador in the last 32. Continue reading...
items.content: Three of the victims died from suffocationMore than a million people gathered in Mexico CityFour people died, three ⁠from suffocation, as thousands of fans crowded Mexico City streets during World Cup celebrations, the capital’s health secretariat ⁠said in the early ⁠hours ​of Wednesday.The deaths occurred near the Angel of Independence landmark, where thousands of fans had gathered to ⁠celebrate Mexico’s 2-0 victory over Ecuador in the last 32. Continue reading...

clusters.title: U.S.-Iran talks pause for ayatollah's funeral after claims of progress

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera

items.title: U.S.-Iran talks pause for ayatollah's funeral after claims of progress
items.standfirst: Negotiations between Iran and the U.S. will pause for a week for the funeral of Iran's slain supreme leader, but Trump, and mediators, say there's been progress.
items.content: Negotiations between Iran and the U.S. will pause for a week for the funeral of Iran's slain supreme leader, but Trump, and mediators, say there's been progress.

items.title: US-Iran talks in Doha: What were the outcomes and what’s next?
items.standfirst: Qatar says the next meeting will take place after funeral processions for Iran's former supreme leader.
items.content: Qatar says the next meeting will take place after funeral processions for Iran's former supreme leader.

clusters.title: No 10 shares ‘public’s shock’ at reports of convicted people smuggler living in UK
clusters.neutral_headline: People smuggler convicted in France found by BBC living in UK and seeking asylum
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: No 10 shares ‘public’s shock’ at reports of convicted people smuggler living in UK
items.standfirst: Downing Street urgently looking into BBC report that ‘godfather’ of Calais migrant camps is living in LeicestershireDowning Street has said it shares “the public’s shock” at a report that a convicted people smuggler is living in Britain and is urgently looking into the circumstances.The man, once labelled “the godfather” of the Calais migrant camps, was tracked down by the BBC to Leicestershire, where he reportedly changed his name from Twana Jamal and was working illegally while attempting to claim asylum. Continue reading...
items.content: Downing Street urgently looking into BBC report that ‘godfather’ of Calais migrant camps is living in LeicestershireDowning Street has said it shares “the public’s shock” at a report that a convicted people smuggler is living in Britain and is urgently looking into the circumstances.The man, once labelled “the godfather” of the Calais migrant camps, was tracked down by the BBC to Leicestershire, where he reportedly changed his name from Twana Jamal and was working illegally while attempting to claim asylum. Continue reading...

items.title: People smuggler convicted in France found by BBC living in UK and seeking asylum
items.standfirst: Once described as "the godfather" of French migrant camps, he has been working in a Leicestershire village.
items.content: Once described as "the godfather" of French migrant camps, he has been working in a Leicestershire village.

items.content: Trump was more 'funny' than he had been expecting, Morgan McSweeney told the BBC.

items.content: Wave of arrests made across South Africa as xenophobic tensions erupt into nationwide marches.

items.standfirst: Thousands have fled South Africa, fearing they will be attacked or killed, after an anti-immigrant movement set a “deadline” for them to leave the country.
items.content: Thousands have fled South Africa, fearing they will be attacked or killed, after an anti-immigrant movement set a “deadline” for them to leave the country.
